title: Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024
subtitle: Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to [national estimates](#dod:ilo-national-estimates) and may not be fully comparable across countries.
caption:
Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024

Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to national estimates and may not be fully comparable across countries.

This scatter plot compares female employment rates in 1980 against 2024 for a selection of countries, with the vertical axis representing 1980 values and the horizontal axis representing 2024 values. Both axes range from 0% to 100%. Circles are sized by population, and colors denote regions: North America (orange), South America (maroon), Africa (purple), Europe (blue), Asia (teal), and Oceania (light blue). The Bahamas sits in the far right of the chart, indicating a 2024 rate near 90%, while maintaining a 1980 rate around 25%. Nigeria appears in the upper right quadrant with a 2024 rate near 80% and a 1980 rate around 35%. Malawi is positioned high on the vertical axis near 90% for 1980, with a 2024 value around 35%. Thailand and Burkina Faso show 1980 rates between 70% and 80%, with 2024 rates near 60%. The United States, Brazil, and the United Kingdom cluster in the center, with 2024 rates between 50% and 60% and 1980 rates between 40% and 50%. European nations like Italy, Spain, and the Netherlands group in the lower-middle section, showing 2024 rates between 50% and 60% and 1980 rates between 25% and 35%. Bangladesh and Pakistan sit in the lower left, with 2024 rates between 20% and 40% and 1980 rates below 15%. Employment is defined in line with the 13th ICLS and includes the production of goods for own use, such as subsistence farming.
